skill.name: onboarding-cro
skill.description: Expert guidance for optimizing post-signup onboarding, user activation, first-run experience, and time-to-value.
skill.summary: A comprehensive skill for designing onboarding flows that drive user activation and long-term retention.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill consists entirely of markdown documentation providing onboarding optimization guidance. All static analysis findings were false positives: external_command patterns appeared in documentation code examples (not executable), cryptographic warnings were incorrect detections on plain text content. No executable code, network access, or system commands present.

content.user_title: Optimize your user onboarding and activation flow
content.value_statement: Poor onboarding causes users to churn before experiencing your product's value. This skill provides proven frameworks to guide users to their aha moment quickly and build lasting engagement habits.
actual_capabilities: Assess current onboarding flow and identify drop-off points
Define activation metrics and aha moment for your product
Design onboarding checklists, empty states, and guided tours
Create trigger-based email sequences for onboarding
Build engagement loops and milestone celebration patterns
Provide experiment ideas for flow optimization
limitations: Cannot directly implement onboarding UI changes in your product
Requires user to provide context about their product and current metrics
Recommendations are strategic and require manual implementation
Does not integrate with analytics tools for live data

use_cases.title: SaaS startup improving activation rate
use_cases.description: A B2B SaaS company struggling with low activation rates uses this skill to redesign their onboarding flow, implement a progress checklist, and establish trigger-based email sequences.

use_cases.title: Mobile app reducing early churn
use_cases.description: A mobile app experiencing high day-1 churn uses this skill to optimize permission request timing, create a quick first-session win, and establish habit loops for daily engagement.
use_cases.target_user: Mobile product owners and UX designers

use_cases.title: Marketplace accelerating first transaction
use_cases.description: A two-sided marketplace uses this skill to streamline profile completion, guide first search and transaction, and create engagement loops that drive repeat activity on both sides.

prompt_templates.title: Basic onboarding audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current onboarding flow. I have a [product type] with [describe signup process]. Users currently drop off at [specific step]. Analyze potential issues and suggest improvements.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have an existing onboarding flow and want to identify weaknesses.

prompt_templates.title: Define activation metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define my product's activation event. My product is a [product type] that helps users [value proposition]. Retained users typically [describe behavior]. What action should I track as my aha moment?
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to identify the key activation event to optimize for.

prompt_templates.title: Design onboarding checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an onboarding checklist for my [product type]. Key setup steps include [list steps]. My users are [target audience]. Order by value and provide microcopy for each item.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to implement a progress-based checklist pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-channel onboarding strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a coordinated onboarding experience across in-app and email for my [product type]. Key events are [list events]. Create trigger definitions, timing, and content recommendations for each channel.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a comprehensive multi-channel onboarding approach.

output_examples.input: I run a project management SaaS. Users sign up with email, then see a blank dashboard. Only 30% create their first project within 24 hours.
output_examples.output: Finding: Blank slate overwhelm causes early drop-off. Recommendation: Implement a guided first-project wizard with a template gallery. Pre-populate with 3 common use cases (Marketing Campaign, Product Launch, Team Goals). Add a progress bar showing 25% complete when project is created.

output_examples.input: Our mobile fitness app asks for health permissions immediately on first launch. 40% of users deny permissions and never return.
output_examples.output: Finding: Permission request timing creates friction before value demonstration. Recommendation: Move health permission request to after the first workout completion. Users who experience value are 3x more likely to grant permissions. Add an explainer screen showing what the permissions enable.

best_practices: Get users to their aha moment before asking for signup when possible
Focus the first session on one successful outcome, not feature education
Show progress early and celebrate milestone completions
Use interactive doing instead of tutorial watching
Coordinate email and in-app messages without duplicating content
anti_patterns: Long setup wizards before users experience any value
Showing all features at once instead of progressive disclosure
Dead-end screens without clear next actions
Asking for permissions or credit cards before demonstrating value
Generic onboarding that ignores user role or use case

faq.question: How do I identify my product's aha moment?
faq.answer: Analyze what action retained users complete that churned users do not. Look for the earliest behavioral indicator correlating with long-term retention. Common patterns: first content created, first teammate invited, first value-generating action completed.

faq.question: Should onboarding be optional or required?
faq.answer: Make onboarding skippable but compelling. Users who feel trapped often rebel. Instead, design onboarding so the path of least resistance leads through key activation steps. Provide clear value at each step to encourage voluntary completion.

faq.question: How long should an onboarding flow be?
faq.answer: As short as possible while achieving activation. Focus on the minimum steps needed for users to experience core value. Complex products may need multi-day onboarding spread across sessions. Measure time-to-activation, not just completion rate.

faq.question: When should I send onboarding emails?
faq.answer: Use behavior-based triggers, not fixed schedules. Send welcome email immediately, then trigger based on actions (or inaction). Example: incomplete setup after 24 hours, activation achieved (celebration), stalled user after 3 days of inactivity.

faq.question: How do I measure onboarding success?
faq.answer: Track activation rate (percentage reaching aha moment), time-to-activation, and day 1/7/30 retention by cohort. Also monitor funnel drop-off at each step to identify optimization opportunities. Segment by user source and role.

faq.question: Should I use a product tour or interactive walkthrough?
faq.answer: Interactive walkthroughs typically outperform passive tours. Let users learn by doing actual tasks in context. If using tours, keep them under 5 steps, make them dismissible, and trigger them when users encounter new features naturally.
